name: ncsn

members.name: NCSNConfig
members.signature: NCSNConfig(sample_size: int | tuple[int, int] = 32, in_channels: int = 3, out_channels: int = 3, act_fn: GenerativeActivation = 'silu', base_channels: int = 128, channel_mult: tuple[int, ...] = (lambda: (1, 2, 2, 2))(), num_res_blocks: int = 2, attention_resolutions: tuple[int, ...] = (lambda: (16,))(), num_heads: int = 1, dropout: float = 0.0, resnet_groups: int = 32, num_noise_levels: int = 10, sigma_max: float = 50.0, sigma_min: float = 0.01, langevin_steps: int = 100, langevin_eps: float = 2e-05)

methods.name: __init__
methods.signature: __init__(sample_size: int | tuple[int, int] = 32, in_channels: int = 3, out_channels: int = 3, act_fn: GenerativeActivation = 'silu', base_channels: int = 128, channel_mult: tuple[int, ...] = (lambda: (1, 2, 2, 2))(), num_res_blocks: int = 2, attention_resolutions: tuple[int, ...] = (lambda: (16,))(), num_heads: int = 1, dropout: float = 0.0, resnet_groups: int = 32, num_noise_levels: int = 10, sigma_max: float = 50.0, sigma_min: float = 0.01, langevin_steps: int = 100, langevin_eps: float = 2e-05)

parameters.name: base_channels
parameters.description: First U-Net stage width (paper: 128).

parameters.name: channel_mult
parameters.description:
Per-stage channel multiplier.  Same semantics as
`DDPMConfig`.

parameters.name: num_res_blocks
parameters.description: ResBlocks per stage.

parameters.name: attention_resolutions
parameters.description:
Spatial sizes at which to insert
self-attention.

parameters.name: num_heads
parameters.description: Attention head count.

parameters.name: dropout
parameters.description: Dropout inside ResBlocks.

parameters.name: resnet_groups
parameters.description: GroupNorm group count.

parameters.name: num_noise_levels
parameters.description:
Number of σ levels `L` (paper L=10, NCSNv2 large
L ≈ 200–500).

parameters.name: sigma_max
parameters.description: Largest noise σ_1 (paper CIFAR-10: 50.0).

parameters.name: sigma_min
parameters.description: Smallest noise σ_L (paper CIFAR-10: 0.01).

parameters.name: langevin_steps
parameters.description: Sampler steps per σ level (paper T=100).

parameters.name: langevin_eps
parameters.description:
Sampler step-size base (paper ε=2e-5).  Per-σ step
size is `ε · σ_i² / σ_L²` (Song 2019 §4.3).

members.name: NCSNForImageGeneration

methods.name: forward
methods.extended:
For each image we sample `i ~ Uniform({0, …, L-1})`, perturb
`x̃ = x + σ_i · z` with `z ~ N(0, I)`, and minimise

L = ½ · E ‖ σ_i · s_θ(x̃, i) + z ‖²

which is the σ-weighted DSM loss.

methods.name: generate
methods.signature: generate(n_samples: int = 1, langevin_steps: int | None = None, return_intermediates: bool = False, device: str = 'cpu')
methods.extended:
Initialises `x ~ N(0, σ_max² I)` then iterates over the σ
schedule from largest to smallest noise.  At each σ_i takes
`langevin_steps` Langevin steps with step size
`α_i = ε · σ_i² / σ_L²`:

x ← x + (α_i / 2) · s_θ(x, i) + √α_i · z      z ~ N(0, I)

parameters.name: n_samples
parameters.description: Batch size of the generated images.

parameters.name: langevin_steps
parameters.description:
Override `config.langevin_steps` for this
                 call (typically lowered in unit tests).

parameters.name: return_intermediates
parameters.description:
If True, also returns one sample per σ
                  level (after its inner Langevin loop).

parameters.name: device
parameters.description: Where to allocate the initial noise.

returns.description: class:`GenerationOutput` with the final `(n_samples, C, H, W)`

members.summary: NCSN with the DSM training loss and annealed Langevin `.generate()`.
members.extended:
Wraps `NCSNModel` with denoising score matching (DSM) for
training and annealed Langevin dynamics for inference.  Sampling does
**not** reuse `DiffusionMixin.generate` because NCSN's nested
(per-$\sigma$ x per-step) loop has different semantics from the
DDPM scheduler step.

Training contract
    `forward(x)` samples a random noise-level index per image,
    perturbs `x` accordingly, runs the score network, and returns
    the DSM loss (Song 2019 Eq. 6).

Sampling
    `generate(n_samples)` runs annealed Langevin dynamics across the
    full $\sigma$ schedule (Song 2019 Algorithm 1).

parameters.name: config
parameters.description:
Hyperparameters controlling architecture, noise schedule, and
Langevin sampler.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.generative.ncsn import (
...     NCSNConfig, NCSNForImageGeneration,
... )
>>> cfg = NCSNConfig(sample_size=32, base_channels=32,
...                  channel_mult=(1, 2), num_res_blocks=1,
...                  resnet_groups=16, num_noise_levels=10,
...                  langevin_steps=2)
>>> model = NCSNForImageGeneration(cfg).eval()
>>> x = lucid.randn((1, 3, 32, 32))
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.sample.shape, out.loss.shape   # score field + scalar loss
((1, 3, 32, 32), ())
notes:
Reference: Song and Ermon, *"Generative Modeling by Estimating
Gradients of the Data Distribution"*, NeurIPS, 2019 ([arXiv:1907.05600](https://arxiv.org/abs/1907.05600));
NCSNv2 refinements in Song and Ermon, 2020 ([arXiv:2006.09011](https://arxiv.org/abs/2006.09011)).

Denoising score matching loss:



$$
\mathcal{L}_{\text{DSM}}(\theta)
    = \tfrac{1}{2}\,
      \mathbb{E}_{\sigma, x, \tilde{x}}\!\left[
        \big\lVert
            \sigma\, s_\theta(\tilde{x}, \sigma) + z
        \big\rVert^2
      \right],
$$

with $\tilde{x} = x + \sigma z$, $z \sim \mathcal{N}(0, \mathbf{I})$.

attributes.name: sigmas
attributes.description:
Buffer of shape `(num_noise_levels,)` holding the geometric
$\sigma$ schedule (mirrored from `self.ncsn.sigmas` for
convenience in loss / sampling code).

members.name: NCSNModel

methods.name: forward
methods.summary: Predict the score at `sample` for the given noise-level indices.

parameters.name: sample
parameters.description: `(B, in_channels, H, W)` perturbed image `x̃`.

parameters.name: sigma_idx
parameters.description: `(B,)` integer indices into the σ schedule.

returns.description: class:`DiffusionModelOutput` whose `sample` field is the raw

members.summary: Score network $s_\theta(\tilde{x}, \sigma_i)$ over a geometric $\sigma$ schedule.
members.extended:
Implements the noise-conditional score network of Song and Ermon, 2019
(and the NCSNv2 / NCSN++ refinements of Song 2020, Song et al. 2021).
Reuses `DDPMUNet` as the score backbone — modern NCSN variants
converged on the same U-Net architecture as diffusion — but feeds the
integer noise-level index $i \in [0, L)$ rather than a raw
$\sigma$ value through the timestep embedding (matching the
NCSNv2 / NCSN++ recipe).  The geometric $\sigma$ table itself is
held as a non-persistent buffer so it travels with `.to(device=...)`.

parameters.name: config
parameters.description:
Hyperparameters controlling the U-Net topology
(`base_channels`, `channel_mult`, `num_res_blocks`,
`attention_resolutions`, `num_heads`, `dropout`,
`resnet_groups`) and the score-based extras
(`num_noise_levels`, `sigma_max`, `sigma_min`).

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.generative.ncsn import NCSNConfig, NCSNModel
>>> cfg = NCSNConfig(sample_size=32, base_channels=32,
...                  channel_mult=(1, 2), num_res_blocks=1,
...                  resnet_groups=16, num_noise_levels=10)
>>> model = NCSNModel(cfg).eval()
>>> x_tilde = lucid.randn((1, 3, 32, 32))
>>> sigma_idx = lucid.tensor([3]).long()
>>> out = model(x_tilde, sigma_idx)
>>> out.sample.shape   # (1, 3, 32, 32) — raw score
(1, 3, 32, 32)
notes:
Reference: Song and Ermon, *"Generative Modeling by Estimating
Gradients of the Data Distribution"*, NeurIPS, 2019
([arXiv:1907.05600](https://arxiv.org/abs/1907.05600)); NCSNv2 refinements in Song and Ermon, 2020
([arXiv:2006.09011](https://arxiv.org/abs/2006.09011)).

The trained score satisfies



$$
s_\theta(\tilde{x}, \sigma)
    \;\approx\; \nabla_{\tilde{x}} \log p_\sigma(\tilde{x}),
$$

where $p_\sigma$ is the data distribution convolved with a
Gaussian of standard deviation $\sigma$.

attributes.name: unet
attributes.description:
Shared U-Net backbone instantiated from a derived
`DDPMConfig`.

attributes.name: sigmas
attributes.description:
Buffer of shape `(num_noise_levels,)` holding the geometric
$\sigma_1 > \sigma_2 > \cdots > \sigma_L$ schedule.

members.name: ncsn_celeba
members.summary: Construct an NCSN score network for the CelebA 64x64 setup.
members.extended:
Paper-faithful CelebA 64x64 configuration from NCSNv2 (Song 2020)
Appendix C: sample size 64x64, `base_channels=128`,
`channel_mult=(1, 1, 2, 2, 4, 4)`, 2 ResBlocks per stage,
self-attention at the 16x16 feature map, $L = 500$ geometric
noise levels in $[\sigma_{\min} = 0.01, \sigma_{\max} = 90]$,
and the NCSNv2 Langevin schedule ($T = 5$ steps per level,
$\epsilon = 2 \cdot 10^{-5}$).

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description: Reserved for future weight registration; currently a no-op.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description:
Optional `NCSNConfig` field overrides forwarded into the
underlying config.

returns.description:
Score network configured with the CelebA 64x64 setup and any
overrides.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.generative.ncsn import ncsn_celeba
>>> model = ncsn_celeba().eval()
>>> x_tilde = lucid.randn((1, 3, 64, 64))
>>> sigma_idx = lucid.tensor([10]).long()
>>> out = model(x_tilde, sigma_idx)
>>> out.sample.shape   # (1, 3, 64, 64)
(1, 3, 64, 64)
notes:
Reference: Song and Ermon, *"Improved Techniques for Training
Score-Based Generative Models"* (NCSNv2), NeurIPS, 2020
([arXiv:2006.09011](https://arxiv.org/abs/2006.09011)), Appendix C.

members.name: ncsn_celeba_gen
members.summary: Construct an NCSN CelebA 64x64 model with DSM loss and `.generate()`.
members.extended:
Same trunk as `ncsn_celeba` (CelebA 64x64 setup, $L = 500$
noise levels), wrapped with the denoising score-matching loss and
annealed Langevin sampling.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description: Reserved for future weight registration; currently a no-op.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description:
Optional `NCSNConfig` field overrides forwarded into the
underlying config.

returns.description:
CelebA 64x64 NCSN wrapped with the DSM loss head and Langevin
sampler.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.generative.ncsn import ncsn_celeba_gen
>>> model = ncsn_celeba_gen().eval()
>>> x = lucid.randn((1, 3, 64, 64))
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.sample.shape   # raw score field
(1, 3, 64, 64)
notes:
Reference: Song and Ermon, *"Improved Techniques for Training
Score-Based Generative Models"* (NCSNv2), NeurIPS, 2020
([arXiv:2006.09011](https://arxiv.org/abs/2006.09011)), Appendix C.

members.name: ncsn_cifar
members.summary: Construct an NCSN score network for the CIFAR-10 setup.
members.extended:
Paper-faithful CIFAR-10 configuration combining Song and Ermon, 2019
§4.1 with the NCSNv2 (Song 2020) Table 1 refinements: sample size
32x32, `base_channels=128`, `channel_mult=(1, 2, 2, 2)`, 2
ResBlocks per stage, self-attention at the 16x16 feature map,
$L = 232$ geometric noise levels in $[\sigma_{\min} = 0.01, \sigma_{\max} = 50]$, and the NCSNv2 §3.3 Langevin schedule
($T = 5$ steps per level, $\epsilon = 2 \cdot 10^{-5}$).

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description: Reserved for future weight registration; currently a no-op.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description:
Optional `NCSNConfig` field overrides forwarded into the
underlying config.

returns.description:
Score network configured with the CIFAR-10 setup and any
overrides.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.generative.ncsn import ncsn_cifar
>>> model = ncsn_cifar().eval()
>>> x_tilde = lucid.randn((1, 3, 32, 32))
>>> sigma_idx = lucid.tensor([3]).long()
>>> out = model(x_tilde, sigma_idx)
>>> out.sample.shape   # (1, 3, 32, 32) — raw score
(1, 3, 32, 32)

members.name: ncsn_cifar_gen
members.summary: Construct an NCSN CIFAR-10 model with DSM loss and `.generate()`.
members.extended:
Same trunk as `ncsn_cifar` (CIFAR-10 setup, $L = 232$
noise levels), wrapped with the denoising score-matching loss and
annealed Langevin sampling.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description: Reserved for future weight registration; currently a no-op.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description:
Optional `NCSNConfig` field overrides forwarded into the
underlying config.

returns.description: CIFAR-10 NCSN wrapped with the DSM loss head and Langevin sampler.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.generative.ncsn import ncsn_cifar_gen
>>> model = ncsn_cifar_gen().eval()
>>> x = lucid.randn((1, 3, 32, 32))
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.sample.shape   # raw score field
(1, 3, 32, 32)
notes:
Reference: Song and Ermon, NeurIPS, 2019 ([arXiv:1907.05600](https://arxiv.org/abs/1907.05600)); NCSNv2 in
Song and Ermon, 2020 ([arXiv:2006.09011](https://arxiv.org/abs/2006.09011)).

summary: NCSN family — Song & Ermon, 2019 (score-based generative models).
citation: Song, Yang, and Stefano Ermon. "Generative Modeling by Estimating Gradients of the Data Distribution." NeurIPS, 2019.
theory:
NCSN — *Noise-Conditional Score Network* — is the score-based
counterpart of DDPM.  Rather than predicting noise inside a discrete
Markov chain, it directly learns the **(Stein) score function**
$s_\theta(x, \sigma) \approx \nabla_x \log p_\sigma(x)$, where
$p_\sigma$ is the data distribution convolved with Gaussian
noise of standard deviation $\sigma$.

Training uses **denoising score matching** (Vincent 2011): for a
noised sample
$\tilde{x} = x + \sigma \epsilon,\; \epsilon \sim \mathcal{N}(0, \mathbf{I})$, the conditional score is available in
closed form,
$\nabla_{\tilde{x}} \log p_\sigma(\tilde{x} \mid x) = -(\tilde{x} - x)/\sigma^2$, giving the loss



$$
\mathcal{L}_{\text{DSM}}(\theta)
    = \mathbb{E}_{\sigma, x, \tilde{x}}\!\left[
        \frac{1}{2}\, \lambda(\sigma)
        \left\lVert
            s_\theta(\tilde{x}, \sigma)
            + \frac{\tilde{x} - x}{\sigma^2}
        \right\rVert^2
      \right],
$$

with the standard weighting $\lambda(\sigma) = \sigma^2$ so
every noise level contributes comparable magnitude.  The
architecture reuses the same U-Net as DDPM but conditions on
$\sigma$ (continuous) rather than a discrete timestep
$t$.

The key innovation is **noise annealing**: a geometric schedule
$\sigma_1 > \sigma_2 > \cdots > \sigma_L$ (defaults
$\sigma_1 = 50$, $\sigma_L = 0.01$, $L = 10$)
where the largest $\sigma_1$ smooths the data distribution
enough that low-density regions become traversable, then sampling
refines toward $\sigma_L \approx 0$ where the score
approximates that of clean data.  Sampling uses **annealed Langevin
dynamics** — for each level $i$ in order, run $T_i$
steps of



$$
x \leftarrow x + \frac{\alpha_i}{2}\, s_\theta(x, \sigma_i)
    + \sqrt{\alpha_i}\; z,
\qquad z \sim \mathcal{N}(0, \mathbf{I}),
$$

with the per-level step size
$\alpha_i = \epsilon \cdot \sigma_i^2 / \sigma_L^2$ (Song
2019, §4.3) — large early on, decaying as noise shrinks.  The
score-based view unifies with diffusion through the **continuous
SDE framework** (Song et al., 2021), where DDPM and NCSN become
different discretisations of the same reverse-time process.
